channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: You Can Pre-Order GTA 6 Right Now in New Zealand
item.content:encoded:
If you live in New Zealand, I know this is very specific, then you don’t need to wait any longer: You can pre-order GTA 6 right now.

There’s no sneaky New Zealand trick here, before you start asking questions. If you’re based outside of New Zealand, you’ve got a few more hours to wait until you can finally lock in your GTA 6 purchase.

Grand Theft Auto 6 Ready For Pre-Order Now

As you can see here from @GameRoll_, while it’s officially June 24 everywhere else, the time difference means New Zealand gets first dibs on pre-orders!

You can pre-order either the normal edition or the more expensive GTA 6 Ultimate Edition—which includes a lot of stuff, by the way.

  • More Insider Gaming: GTA 6 Has Practically No Loading Screens on PS5

Once you’ve locked in your purchase, you’re all set.

You should be able to preload GTA 6 close to its November 19 release date, and the game we thought would never come out (and still could get delayed, you never know at this point) will be yours.

In the next 24 hours, every other country and region will be able to pre-order GTA 6.

Grand Theft Auto 6’s base edition will cost $80, and you’ll also have a dilemma over the game’s controversial physical edition issue.

  • More Insider Gaming: GTA 6 is a ‘Single-Player Experience’ With No Mention of GTA Online

If you’re not in the right region to pre-order yet, kill some time with GTA 6 Trailer 2.

Will you be pre-ordering GTA 6 as soon as you get the chance? Let us know your guesses through the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, check out the interesting find that GTA 6 is a ‘Single-Player Experience’ with no mention of GTA Online, and don’t forget to sign up for our Insider Gaming Newsletter.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: GTA 6 Will Be PS5 Pro Enhanced
item.content:encoded:
Grand Theft Auto 6 pre-orders have officially begun rolling out, with New Zealand players among the first to gain access thanks to the region’s midnight launch schedule. As fans pore over the newly updated store pages, one small detail has seemingly confirmed that GTA 6 will be PS5 Pro Enhanced.

The game has already been marketed as ‘Best on PS5,’ with Sony highlighting features such as the DualSense controller and 3D AudioTech as leading factors, with practically no loading screens stealing the show. However, the PS5 Pro version could scale things up to 11, further cementing PlayStation as the best place to play GTA 6.

What Will GTA 6 PS5 Pro Enhancements Look Like?

Images of the updated PlayStation Store page have begun circulating online, and alongside the option to finally pre-order the game, fans have spotted the PS5 Pro Enhanced tag attached to GTA 6.

Exactly what those enhancements entail remains unclear, but based on other PS5 Pro-supported titles, such as Resident Evil 9, players can likely expect improvements to both image quality and performance through PSSR. Many are already hoping the enhanced hardware will allow GTA 6 to achieve a stable 60fps while maintaining near-4K visual fidelity.

With prices rising for the PS5 Pro, if you don’t already have one, it’ll set you back a whopping $749. It’s up to you whether playing GTA 6 in the best possible way is worth that price, but until it comes to PC, that’ll likely be where it plays best.

  • Related: Everything Included In GTA 6 Ultimate Edition & Price

For now, though, Rockstar and Sony have yet to outline the specifics, meaning any discussion around frame rates and graphical upgrades remains speculative until an official announcement is made.

What isn’t speculation is the sheer amount of conversation surrounding the game. Alongside the PS5 Pro news, fans have been debating Rockstar’s decision to lock exclusive single-player content behind the Ultimate Edition, while physical media enthusiasts were disappointed to learn that physical copies of GTA 6 will contain a download code rather than a game disc.

Everyone expected GTA 6 to dominate headlines once pre-orders opened, and that’s exactly what’s happened. With launch day drawing closer and a price now confirmed, it feels like the industry has officially entered the GTA 6 era. Join in with the hype and give the second trailer another view below:

Do you have a PS5 Pro? Where will you be playing GTA 6? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more GTA 6 news, read about the cost of the base game, and don’t forget to sign up for our Newsletter.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.title: You’d Be Insane Not To Pay $100 for Grand Theft Auto 6 Now
item.content:encoded:
Rockstar Games and Take-Two Interactive have finally lifted the lid on the Grand Theft Auto 6 price after many months of speculation, and what they’ve revealed pretty much gels with most expectations. The base game is set to cost $80, but for just $20 more ($100 total, for those who hate math), you can get the Ultimate Edition.

Fortunately, Rockstar Games has also revealed what you get when you buy that $100 Ultimate Edition, and there’s so much content packed into the bundle that you’d be something of a fool not to pay $100 for Grand Theft Auto 6.

GTA 6 Ultimate Edition Should Be Standard

The base edition of Grand Theft Auto 6 comes with no frills and no bells on, but if you pre-order it, you’ll get a nostalgia-fuelled throwback pack themed around GTA: Vice City. You’ll be paying $80 to experience what might be the most popular game of all time, or at least the fastest-selling game, but that’s about it.

However, chuck an extra twenty bucks on your purchase, and you’ll walk away with the Ultimate Edition.

As revealed earlier by Rockstar Games, the Ultimate Edition comes with a wealth of content, including some exclusive elements that standard edition players simply won’t be able to access.

  • RELATED: GTA 6 File Size Image is Likely Fake

For example, five in-game stores are accessible only to those who buy the Ultimate Edition. This includes a tattoo parlour, a car mod shop, a hair salon, and a clothing store. It stands to reason that their entire function will be rendered inaccessible to standard owners, writing off a fair chunk of customization content.

Additionally, there are exclusive side missions tied to the Ultimate Edition, including a gang compound bursting with high-tier loot and a classic car collection function. That latter bit has me the most interested, as this ‘special commission’ allows you to find and restore project cars and ‘barn finds’, much like you can in games like Forza Horizon 6.

There’s more, of course. Those partaking in the GTA 6 Ultimate Edition will get exclusive vehicles, weapons, weapon skins, outfits, styles, vehicle modkits, hideout elements, and entire bundles of content, all wrapped up with that $20 boost.

Folks have been waiting more than a decade for GTA 6. It has been a long time since Grand Theft Auto 5 was released in 2013, and the die-hard fans out there will have already earmarked their next $100 for that pre-order, because for that extra $20, you get so much content, some of which might be considered substantial.

If you’ve been waiting this long for GTA 6, why wouldn’t you pay the extra $20? We were expecting the Ultimate Edition (or something akin to that) to run up a bill of around $150, so this is a steal.

Admittedly, it’s missing a few things. There are no early access elements for GTA 6, and even buying the game physically won’t net you a disc, as there’s only a code in the box. It’s a fair bet that there’s nothing physical attached to the Ultimate Edition, which is a shame. In 2013, the Ultimate Edition of GTA 5 at least netted you a nice physical map and a steelbook for that collector-focused edge.

But what do you think? Will you be investing $100 in Grand Theft Auto 6 to get all the extra content, or are you just not that bothered? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more gaming news, check out the interesting find that GTA 6 is a ‘Single-Player Experience’ with no mention of GTA Online, and don’t forget to sign up for our Insider Gaming newsletter

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: Hideo Kojima Wants OD to go ‘Beyond The Limit’ of Scariness
item.content:encoded:
Hideo Kojima has big plans for his upcoming horror title OD and wants to go ‘beyond the limit’ of scariness, pushing things further than any video game has before.

Initially announced in 2023, OD has big names in the cast, including Sophia Lillis, Hunter Schafer, and Udo Kier, and boasts involvement from Jordan Peele, who is behind films like Get Out and Nope.

While scary video games have tended to remain fairly tame and not push things too far into the nightmare realm, Kojima wants to go bigger and scarier than anyone before.

Kojima Wants OD to be ‘as Scary as Possible’

In an interview with Entertainment Weekly, Kojima revealed how many big companies turned down his proposal, who said he was “crazy” and that they “really don’t understand the concept,” before Xbox ultimately got on board.

Kojima explained he planned to “go beyond the limit of the ‘scariness’ that other games had reached,” with the title set as “a single-player game” that he wanted to make “as scary as possible.”

That may prove to be too much for some players, and Kojima is making OD more accommodating by devising a system that allows players to continue if things get too scary, though he “can’t say much more, because it’ll give too much of a hint on the system.”

  • Related: Asha Sharma Suggests XBOX Might Discover ‘The Next Kojima’

No release window for OD has been revealed as of yet, and it could still be a few more years in the making before gamers get thrust into the narrative nightmare.

Check out a teaser trailer for OD below.

Are you excited for Kojima to let fear run loose in OD? Let us know in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For your next, take a look at all the Ultimate Edition and pre-order bonuses for GTA 6, and don’t forget to subscribe to our newsletter.
